title: salix depressa subsp. rostrata
intro: Salix depressa subsp. rostrata, also known as Goat Willow or Weeping Willow, is a subspecies of willow tree that is native to Northern Europe. The bark of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of salicylic acid. The health benefits of Salix depressa subsp. rostrata include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into a bark infusion or decoction, which can be used to treat a variety of conditions, including arthritis, fever, and digestive issues.
